sglang.title: Fix AssertionError crash in disagg prefill inflight queue with PP
sglang.author: dubin555
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fixes #20485
In pipeline-parallel (PP) disaggregated prefill, the inflight queue poll can encounter transient states due to cross-rank clock skew. The hard assert and assert False crash the scheduler instead of handling these gracefully.
Changes

Replace the assert poll == Success or poll

Benchmark methodology
I compared main against the patched version that wraps apply_flashinfer_rope_qk_inplace as a compile-friendly custom op.
Performance measurement:

I ran the same five sglang generate commands on both versions.
I added only --perf-dump-path <json> to collect stage-level timings.

sglang.title: Add NCCL/RCCL pre-warming to reduce P99 TTFT cold-start latency
sglang.author: hubertlu-tw
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When using multi-GPU tensor parallelism (TP > 1), the first collective communication operation triggers NCCL/RCCL communicator initialization, causing severe P99 TTFT degradation (up to 1400ms) for the first 2-3 requests.
This PR implements NCCL/RCCL pre-warming during server startup to e

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI] Retrying in case of batch variance effects and reducing flakiness

vllm.bodyExcerpt: On ROCm there is non-determinism in the case of batch sizes greater than 1, and in the case of preemption test, this is exactly what is being tested. Therefore, we add a retry so that any chance of this test failing is met with a second attempt. Furthermore, we disable skinny GEMMs which is particul

vllm.title: Fix text only inputs for MRoPE models with the Transformers modelling backend
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In Transformers v5 text only prompts would fail because mm_token_type_ids is a required positional arg and vLLM was only passing it if there was image data present.
Now, in the Transformers modelling backend, if there is no image data we pass mm_token_type_ids to indicate that all the data is text.

vllm.title: GLM4 tool parser: fix streaming mode

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix GLM4 streaming parser correctness. The parser currently violates the invariant that streamed_args_for_tool and prev_tool_call_arr should be kept in sync which breaks some tool invocations as vLLM's flushing mechanism doesn't work correctly.
There are two violations:

streamed_args_for_to

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Disable cross-layer KV cache for MLA attention backends
vllm.author: haosdent
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #37032
MLA models (e.g., GLM-4.7-Flash) produce garbage output with --kv-offloading-size because cross-layer KV cache allocation creates non-contiguous per-layer views. MLA decode kernels assume contiguous block layout, so they read wrong memory for block_id > 0.
Fix (3 parts)


mla_at

vllm.title: Add simple granite4 tool parser
vllm.author: maxdebayser
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Note: this is a simpler alternative to #35948 based on suggestions by @sfeng33
IBM's Granite 4 models use the Hermes tool calling convention and until now had been using the hermes parser. However, due to the popularity of the Hermes format many additions have been made to this parser to ser

vllm.title: [MTP][Sparse MLA] Take advantage of native MTP support in indexer when possible
vllm.author: MatthewBonanni
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
PR #34552 added support for MTP > 1 with sparse MLA by unconditionally flattening all requests into single-token decodes. The indexer kernel does support MTP = 1, though, and future iterations of the kernel will support other token counts e.g. MTP = 3. This PR takes advantage of this support

vllm.title: [PD][Nixl] Add support for hybrid SSM-FA models
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
For a comprehensive description of the changes proposed here, check out the corresponding RFC #36780.
This PR adds support for hybrid SSM-based models such as nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-FP8 with NixlConnector, enabling KVCache transfer of both FA and Mamba states in disaggregated setups.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix render server crash for quantized models on CPU-only hosts
vllm.author: sagearc
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Description:
Currently, running vllm launch render with a quantized model (like openai/gpt-oss-120b using mxfp4) crashes on CPU-only hosts that lack native bfloat16 support.
This happens because the system auto-detects the mxfp4 quantization and tries to validate it. On hardware without bfloat16 sup

vllm.title: [torch.compile][BE] Modify cudagraph callable to check for is_forward_context_set
vllm.author: Lucaskabela
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
To simplify the multimodal compile UX, we want to enable removing the set_forward_context contextmanager that has been required previously
Why was this ever required?
For the encoder model that ran outside of forward, the forward context was not set in ModelRunner.  This meant that when supp

vllm.title: [Refactor] Consolidate GPT-OSS reasoning parser tests
vllm.author: sfeng33
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Consolidated scattered GPT-OSS structural tag tests from 3 files into a single tests/reasoning/test_gptoss_reasoning_parser.py
Removed redundant test files: test_gptoss_structural_tags_integration.py and test_gptoss_structural_tags.py
Fixed typo: no_func_reaonsing_tag → no_func_reasoning_ta

vllm.author: rasmith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The test_morrio_connector.py tests fail because the transfer_id is not added into the KV transfer params.
This PR adds the transfer_id into the KV transfer params so the tests pass.

 pytest -sv v1/kv_connector/unit/test_moriio_connector.py
Test Result
3 passed, 2 skipped, 3 warning

vllm.title: [Misc] Add `float16` to `CacheDType`
vllm.author: MatthewBonanni
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR adds float16 as an enumerated CacheDType. Many attention backends support float16 in supported_dtypes, and also support auto in supported_kv_cache_dtypes, but don't list float16 in supported_kv_cache_dtypes. This technically means that if someone tried to run a float16 model with --k

vllm.title: [BUGFIX][Mamba] Use uint64 for address in KVBlockZeroer
vllm.author: jikunshang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
a bug fix for https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/35219/changes#diff-645d58630d5acf3a0b07226bfef1e890a584c32502ab97c3d4642070f39a783cR183 ,
for any address related data, we should use uint64 instead of int64.
see previous discussion #25266 (comment)

vllm.title: [Bugfix][MultiConnector] Fix MultiConnector for SupportsHMA sub-connectors
vllm.author: ZhanqiuHu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fixes #36547
MultiConnector.request_finished calls c.request_finished() on all sub-connectors, but NixlConnector (SupportsHMA) only overrides request_finished_all_groups. The call falls through to the base class no-op, so kv_transfer_params is never generated and NIXL transfers silently fail

vllm.title: [compile] Enable mega aot artifact for torch 2.12+.
vllm.author: zhxchen17
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Following up with the previous effort to bundle compiled subgraphs in one artifact, after some local
test we think this should be ready to be enabled with the latest pytorch version by default. This
PR enables the flag with torch 2.12+ and when the toplevel AOT flag is enabled.
Test Plan:
R

vllm.title: [Bugfix] opcheck false mutation error in rms_norm_per_block_quant (#36688)
vllm.author: KrxGu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #36688.
The opcheck call in test_rms_norm was allocating scales with shape
(num_tokens, 1) and passing it to rms_norm_per_block_quant. The blockwise
kernel writes hidden_size / group_size scale values per token, so with 8
groups the buffer was 8× too small. The out-of-bounds writes landed in t

vllm.title: [MoE/EPLB] Fix FlashInfer nvfp4 experts + EPLB correctness
vllm.author: elvircrn
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Two fixes for EPLB + NVFP4 quantization on FlashInfer modular kernels (CuteDSL/CUTLASS/TRTLLM):

Exclude broadcast activation scales from EPLB: w13_input_scale and w2_input_scale are global per-tensor activation scales shared across all experts (created via .expand() with stride 0). They are

vllm.title: [Quant][Feature] Support online MXFP8 quantization for MoE and dense models
vllm.author: EdalatiAli
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add support for online MXFP8 quantization (--quantization mxfp8), enabling BF16/FP16 models to be dynamically quantized to MXFP8 (microscaling FP8 with block-32 scales) at load time — for both linear layers and MoE expert layers.
This is powered by the FlashInfer kernels

trtllm_fp8_block_sc

vllm.title: [Benchmark] Improvements to attention benchmark script
vllm.author: wzhao18
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR improves the attention benchmark in the following areas:

Introduce fp8 kv cache dtype benchmarks for standard attention, MLA and MLA sparse.
Adds CUDA graph support to the benchmark. Our investigation shows that the current measurements can be very off for decode benchmarks due to m

vllm.title: [Doc] Clarify schema enforcement behavior for tool_choice modes
vllm.author: cemigo114
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The tool-calling documentation (docs/features/tool_calling.md) does not explain that tool_choice="auto" does not enforce the function parameter schema during decoding, unlike "required" or named-function modes. Users encountering malformed tool-call arguments in auto mode have no documentati

Old schema:
scaled_fp4_quant(Tensor! output, Tensor input, Tensor! output_scale,
Tensor input_scale, bool is_sf_swizzled_layout) -> ()

scaled

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Add common tool call parser test suite
vllm.author: bbrowning
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This adds a common test suite for tool call parsers and wires all of the existing tool call parsers that had no tests into the common suite. It doesn't yet adapt existing tool call parser tests to fit into the common suite nor augment tool call parsers that already had tests with the new set

vllm.title: [Feature][Frontend] add support for Cohere Embed v2 API
vllm.author: walterbm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Implementation for RFC #37000
Add a Cohere-compatible /v2/embed endpoint (https://docs.cohere.com/reference/embed) to vLLM's pooling API. This is a adapter layer on top of the existing OpenAI /v1/embeddings pipeline to help with code reuse. Any embedding model that already works with with /v

vllm.title: [BugFix] Correct max memory usage for multiple KV-cache groups
vllm.author: peakcrosser7
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR fixes a calculation error in _max_memory_usage_bytes_from_groups() that led to underestimating total memory usage in multi-group cases.
Root Cause
The original implementation only calculated blocks_needed based on the first KV-Cache group (index 0). In models with multiple KV-Cache g

vllm.title: Support non-contiguous KV cache in TRTLLM fp8 dequant kernel
vllm.author: vadiklyutiy
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fix the trtllm_prefill_attn_kvfp8_dequant Triton kernel to support non-contiguous KV cache tensors (e.g. cross-layer unified allocation used by KV offloading).
The kernel previously computed flat pointer offsets from tensor shape, assuming contiguity. With cross-layer KV caches the per-layer

vllm.title: Add ability to replace oot ops when using lora

Purpose
Add ability to support lora when using custom ops. Otherwise, custom ops will not be replaced with Lora layers.

vllm.author: AndreasKaratzas

#36204 replaced the guard filter lambda with torch.compiler.skip_all_guards_unsafe, which doesn't exist in PyTorch < 2.10
Adds a hasattr check to fall back to the equivalent lambda x: [False for _ in x] on older versions
Fixes AttributeError: module 'torch.compiler' has no attribute 'skip_all_guards

The response API tests are scattered in V1 and entrypoint folder, relocate them to centralized location in tests/entrypoints/openai/responses/ for better maintenance.
